summary: Parallel review: FAIL (3 critical, 17 warning, 11 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:420` — `planTasks()` defaults `runbooksDir` to `process.cwd()` instead of receiving project root from caller; latent coupling to execution order. Pass `mainCwd` explicitly.
🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:418` — `planTasks()` has no direct unit test. Task shape mapping and `runbookFlow` return are verified only by code reading.
### Prior 🔴 Resolution
🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:418` — `planTasks()` has no direct unit test; task shape mapping and fallthrough paths verified only by code reading
🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:831` — No CLI E2E test for `--runbook <name>` flag
🟡 SPEC.md:22 — AC says `matchRunbook(description, runbooksDir)` but function takes `runbooks` (array). Parameter name mismatch with Technical Approach.
🟡 SPEC.md:23 — AC wording "narrows candidate set" implies two-phase filtering; actual algorithm is single-phase additive scoring. Misleading AC.
🟡 tasks/task-4-p1-r1/handshake.json — Gate handshake still shows FAIL from earlier broken iteration. Code passes 45/45 tests now. Stale audit trail.
🟡 commit b435764 — Commit message claims `matchRunbook` implementation but diff has zero source changes.
- Prior 🔴 (`selectRunbook` premature abstraction) fully addressed — inlined into `planTasks()`
🟡 `test/runbooks.test.mjs:216` — No test for `score === minScore` boundary; add a test where score exactly equals minScore to verify the `>=` threshold

🟡 `test/runbooks.test.mjs:519` — Integration tests simulate the pipeline manually but never call `planTasks()` directly; actual wiring at `run.mjs:418-451` only exercised through CLI `--dry-run` with non-matching descriptions
🟡 `test/runbooks.test.mjs:576` — `--runbook` flag tested by manual `runbooks.find()`, not through CLI; no integration test passes `--runbook add-cli-command` and asserts output
🟡 `test/runbooks.test.mjs:519` — AC requires console output with matched runbook name and score; no test asserts the `[runbook] matched:` log line

🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs`:110 — `isSafeRegex` heuristic misses alternation-based ReDoS patterns like `(a|a)+$` (verified: 14ms on 20 chars, exponential growth). Low exploitability — requires repo write access to plant malicious YAML, at which point the attacker can already execute arbitrary code via package.json/bin scripts.
- **Premature abstraction**: Prior `selectRunbook` 🔴 verified fixed (inlined into `planTasks`). All remaining abstractions have ≥2 call sites or are 60+ line extractions
- 🟡 `runbooks.mjs:12-78` — Custom YAML parser is correct but becomes a liability if schema grows
- 🟡 `runbooks.mjs:247` — 120-char tie-breaking expression is hard to scan; extract a `tiebreakKey` variable
- 🟡 `runbooks.mjs:194` — `flow` field exercised only by test fixtures, not by any shipped runbook
- 🟡 `runbooks.mjs:258-276` — `include` resolution tested but unused by any shipped runbook
